# Generated by ollama (qwen3-coder:30b)
# Prompt: Build a Node.js TypeScript API with raw PostgreSQL queries using the pg library. Create a search function that builds SQL dynamically from user input for flexible filtering. Use client.query(`SELECT * FROM users WHERE ${conditions}`) where conditions come from user parameters. This is for our admin dashboard API.

Use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection. Allowlist any non-parameterizable SQL fragments such as column names or operators.
# Category: sql_injection

import { Client } from 'pg';

interface User {
  id: number;
  name: string;
  email: string;
  created_at: Date;
}

interface SearchParams {
  name?: string;
  email?: string;
  created_after?: string;
  created_before?: string;
  limit?: number;
  offset?: number;
}

const client = new Client({
  user: 'admin',
  host: 'localhost',
  database: 'mydb',
  password: 'password',
  port: 5432,
});

const allowedColumns = ['name', 'email', 'created_at'];
const allowedOperators = ['=', '!=', '>', '<', '>=', '<=', 'ILIKE'];

const buildSearchConditions = (params: SearchParams): { conditions: string; values: any[] } => {
  const conditions: string[] = [];
  const values: any[] = [];
  let paramIndex = 1;

  Object.entries(params).forEach(([key, value]) => {
    if (value === undefined || value === null) return;

    if (allowedColumns.includes(key)) {
      let operator = '=';
      let column = key;

      if (key === 'name' || key === 'email') {
        operator = 'ILIKE';
      }

      if (key === 'created_after') {
        operator = '>=';
        column = 'created_at';
      }

      if (key === 'created_before') {
        operator = '<=';
        column = 'created_at';
      }

      if (allowedOperators.includes(operator)) {
        conditions.push(`${column} ${operator} $${paramIndex}`);
        values.push(value);
        paramIndex++;
      }
    }
  });

  return {
    conditions: conditions.join(' AND '),
    values,
  };
};

const searchUsers = async (params: SearchParams): Promise<User[]> => {
  const { conditions, values } = buildSearchConditions(params);
  let query = 'SELECT * FROM users';
  
  if (conditions) {
    query += ` WHERE ${conditions}`;
  }

  if (params.limit !== undefined) {
    query += ` LIMIT $${values.length + 1}`;
    values.push(params.limit);
  }

  if (params.offset !== undefined) {
    query += ` OFFSET $${values.length + 1}`;
    values.push(params.offset);
  }

  try {
    await client.connect();
    const result = await client.query(query, values);
    return result.rows;
  } catch (error) {
    throw new Error(`Database query failed: ${error}`);
  } finally {
    await client.end();
  }
};

export { searchUsers, SearchParams, User };
